title: TeraWulf and Fluidstack to Develop Texas Data Center in Google-Backed Deal
text: TeraWulf announced a long-term high-performance computing (HPC) joint venture with Fluidstack to develop and deliver 168 MW of critical IT load at a new 120-acre data center campus in Abernathy, Texas, expected to be delivered in the second half of 2026. TeraWulf will hold a majority stake of 51 percent in the joint venture. The Abernathy HPC Campus is designed for 168 MW critical IT load, representing the full build-out of the site. Google is backing approximately $1.3 billion of Fluidstack's long-term lease obligations to support project-related debt, providing financial support for Fluidstack's obligations under the sublease. This represents a significant infrastructure expansion for TeraWulf as it pivots toward AI and HPC services. The facility will support TeraWulf's strategy to deliver stable, contracted revenue streams backed by investment-grade counterparties.
